title: Product requirements from structured analysis
use_when: Product requirements from structured analysis. Use when the user needs a product workflow for stakeholder management related to product requirements from structured analysis. Trigger terms: product-requirements, product-strategy, collaboration, systems-thinking.
do_not_use_when: Do not use Product requirements from structured analysis when the request is mainly a different lifecycle artifact; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: Product requirements from structured analysis UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
framework_fit: Product requirements from structured analysis fits Delivery work in the Design lifecycle when the user needs Product requirements from structured analysis UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks and has enough product context to make tradeoffs explicit. Use it to produce a decision-ready artifact, not a framework tour.
failure_modes: Produces Product requirements from structured analysis UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks without tying recommendations to the user's Design stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats Product requirements from structured analysis as generic Delivery advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from Product requirements from structured analysis when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $product-requirements-from-structured-analysis to turn this requirements context into a decision-ready Product requirements from structured analysis UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks.
examples.expected_artifact: Product requirements from structured analysis UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
